key_insight: Cayley graphs encode enough geometric and spectral structure to predict several finite-group properties, and graph-aware models can learn this signal effectively.
engineering_relevance: The paper provides a large benchmark of 131,406 labeled Cayley graphs, useful for testing graph analytics, representation learning, and algebra-aware ML pipelines.
method: Built a census of Cayley graphs for all groups of order ≤767 except 512, with exact algebraic labels.
Computed broad graph, cycle, distance, and spectral statistics as engineered features.
Compared classical models, an MLP, and GNNs (notably GIN and, in some fixed-order cases, GCN) for property prediction.
evidence_signal: The dataset spans 131,406 graphs and the abstract reports that engineered graph statistics are highly informative, while GIN and some GCN settings recover substantial structural signal from graph data.
watch_item: Performance may depend strongly on graph order and feature design, so results may not transfer cleanly to larger groups or unseen Cayley-graph families.
paper_title: Learning the Graphical Nature of Symmetries
